rag_rules: Place AGREE contracts in component type annexes for interface behavior and implementation annexes for subcomponent bindings.
Use visible identifiers from the AADL model to ensure type and scope compatibility.
generic_rules: Assumptions and guarantees should reference component ports only in valid scopes.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers not present in the AADL model.
Ensure AGREE expressions align with AADL data types and port directions.
Avoid inserting invented bounds or assumptions not explicitly stated in requirements.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annex blocks are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};`.
Component type contracts should use visible variables and outputs, not implementation-only paths.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type scopes.
Do not assume numeric ranges or event port values without explicit AADL representation.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE validator diagnostics and repair patterns
